config.name: shodh_remember
config.description: Store a new long-term memory for the current character in shodh-memory. Automatically scoped to whichever character is currently speaking -- you never need to specify who it belongs to. Use only for genuinely new information -- if an existing memory is simply being reaffirmed, call shodh_reinforce instead.

content.description: Concise, third-person, self-contained statement of the fact or event to remember (e.g. "The user's dog is named Biscuit and passed away in 2024.").

memoryType.description: One of: fact, preference, event, relationship, promise.

tags.description: A few short keyword tags for later retrieval.

config.name: shodh_recall
config.description: Search shodh-memory for the current character's long-term memories relevant to a query. Automatically scoped to whichever character is currently speaking -- you never need to specify whose memories to search.

query.description: Short keyword query describing what's currently relevant (topic, person mentioned, open question, etc.). Ten words maximum -- keywords, not a full sentence.

limit.description: Max number of memories to return (default 5).

config.name: shodh_reinforce
config.description: Strengthen an existing shodh-memory memory for the current character instead of storing a duplicate. Automatically scoped to whichever character is currently speaking. Use when the character notices a previously stored fact is being reaffirmed rather than learning something new.

content.description: The existing memory/fact being reaffirmed, phrased the same way it would have originally been stored.
